title: 🎙️ Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling
description: Today we explore how Cariboo and rural BC communities create, preserve, and share culture — from festivals and community radio to digital archives and open-source arts tools. We're looking for stories where creativity meets technology in small-town contexts, and where local artists and storytellers are finding new ways to reach audiences beyond geography. - Daily themed article selection for podcast generation

_podcast.theme_description: Today we explore how Cariboo and rural BC communities create, preserve, and share culture — from festivals and community radio to digital archives and open-source arts tools. We're looking for stories where creativity meets technology in small-town contexts, and where local artists and storytellers are finding new ways to reach audiences beyond geography.
_podcast.theme_scoring_prompt: Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling' through a RURAL TECH AND INNOVATION lens. Strong fits include: digital storytelling platforms and tools, community media tech (podcasting, streaming, local publishing), creative tech economy in small towns, rural cultural sector innovation, open-source arts tools, Cariboo arts festivals using digital outreach, preservation of rural cultural heritage through technology, animation/film/media production in rural BC, local radio/podcast infrastructure, and how rural communities use technology for creative expression. Prefer articles that bridge culture AND technology in rural/Cariboo contexts. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match and 0 is completely unrelated.

items.title: [Boing Boing] XOXO festival archived online
items.content_html:
XOXO was a festival for internet artists and creators hosted by Andy McMilland and Andy Baio, held in Portland throughout the 2010s and again in 2014. After more than a decade, it offers "one last thing before we go"—XOXO Explore, a complete archive of every year it took place. — Read the rest

The post XOXO festival archived online appeared first on Boing Boing.

items.title: [Kottke.org] The Film That Attacks You
items.content_html:
For his latest video essay, Evan Puschak tells us about Un Chien Andalou, the pioneering surrealist short film by Luis Buñuel and Salvador Dalí. The film is particularly notable for a shocking shot in the opening scene, which, if you’ve seen it, you’ve likely never forgotten. Said Buñuel of the film:

This film has no intention of attracting nor pleasing the spectator; indeed, on the contrary, it attacks him, to the degree that he belongs to a society with which surrealism is at war.

You can watch Un Chien Andalou on YouTube:

One wonders what Buñuel and Dalí would have made of YouTube…

Tags: art · film school · Luis Buñuel · movies · Salvador Dali · Un Chien Andalou · video

items.title: [Boing Boing] Captain Billy's Whiz Bang gave a pastor's Egypt trip the pulp treatment in 1922
items.content_html:
Captain Billy's Whiz Bang was a humor magazine launched in 1919 by Wilford Hamilton "Captain Billy" Fawcett, a WWI veteran from Minnesota. Named for the artillery shells he'd heard in France, it ran until 1936 and was one of the most popular adult-humor publications of its era: suggestive cartoons, off-color jokes, and material that strained the limits of respectability. — Read the rest

The post Captain Billy's Whiz Bang gave a pastor's Egypt trip the pulp treatment in 1922 appeared first on Boing Boing.

items.title: [New Atlas] Vibrating pillow sleeve warns deaf sleepers of burglars and fires
items.content_html:
A research team supervised by Assoc. Prof. Theo Hughes-Riley from Nottingham Trent University has designed a smart vibrating pillow sleeve prototype, to alert people who are deaf to fire and burglar alarms. The device was recently presented at the Association for Computing Machinery CHI conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems, at the Barcelona International Convention Centre in Spain.

Continue Reading

Category: Good Thinking, Technology

Tags: Nottingham Trent University, Deaf, Sleep, Alert, Hearing Impaired
